channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Magic: The Gathering Marvel Super Heroes Teaser Released
item.content:encoded:
With previews for Magic: The Gathering Marvel Super Heroes set to start next week, Magic: The Gathering head designer Mark Rosewater got ahead of things for his now traditional teaser, which tells us small tidbits about various cards in the main set. 

A couple of notes here before we look at them and speculate a bit with information, is that these are referring only to cards in the main Marvel Super Heroes set, and not Commander decks or Jumpstart exclusive cards. Additionally, the information here is intentionally fractional so we only have partial knowledge. Lastly, Mark Rosewater is a major comic nerd as well, and served as one of the subject matter experts on the design of the set, so there's a decent chance of references to some deep cuts here.

Some creature tokens we can expect:

  • 1/1 Blue Bird Scout,
  • 1/1 Red Alien,
  • 1/1 Green Minion,
  • 2/2 Legendary Green Cat, and
  • 3/3 Colorless Robot Villain (Doombots)

Some interesting new things coming in the set:

  • A new enchantment subtype (probably Plans based on the previewed Doctor Doom),
  • A flavor word that references an "Un-"card (as in from one of the sets Unglued, Unhinged, Unstable or Unfinity),
  • A modal card that lets you choose up to X Modes,
  • A card name in all caps with exclamation marks (seems likely to be onomatopoeia),
  • A creature that can copy three different card types (likely to be creature, artifact and then either land or enchantment. I would wager the former as copying a tree to fit in the background or something like that is very much in the realm of something in comics compared to copying an insubstantial magical effect), and
  • A subtype last seen in a Standard-legal set over two decades ago (perhaps Enchant World)

Mark Rosewater also provided us with some rules text excerpts:

  • “Ward – Get five poison counters.” (Remember it only takes 10 poison for someone to die),
  • “At the beginning of your end step, if you have fewer than ten cards in hand,”
  • “Equip worthy” (Seems like an alternate equip cost for creatures who have something denoting them as worthy and may be for the version of Mjolnir here, unlike the Assassin's Creed version which didn't try to capture that).

Some interesting creature type lines from the set:

  • Legendary Creature – Demigod Warrior Hero (Hi Thor)
  • Legendary Creature – Gamma Doctor Hero (Hi Hulk)
  • Legendary Creature – Alien Dragon Villain
  • Legendary Artifact Creature – Scientist Villain (Hi Doctor Doom? Or Ultron?)
  • Creature – Dragon Cat Dog Bird Frog Hero ( I have no idea )

Finally it was closed out with a list of ten card names we can expect to see in the set:

  • Avengers Assemble!
  • Crowd of True Believers
  • Earth’s Mightiest Heroes 
  • Go Nuts! (Squirrel Girl incoming!)
  • Hex Magic
  • Pym Particles
  • Repulsor Blast
  • Secret Invasion
  • Super-Soldier Serum
  • We Say Thee Nay!

You can read the rest of the teaser, which had a few minor ones I omitted here on Mark Rosewater's blog and stay tuned for our ongoing coverage of Marvel Super Heroes.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don

More Info About This Game

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: White Wolf Announces New Vampire: The Masquerade Project Coming to Gen Con 2026
item.content:encoded:
White Wolf has a new project for Vampire: The Masquerade in the works. The studio now behind the World of Darkness setting stated that new material was in development, and that Gen Con 2026 attendees can get a sneak peek.

The announcement of the new Vampire: The Masquerade project officially happened as part of this year's Darkness Emergent event. During this event, a combination of a massive Vampire: The Masquerade LARP event and community convention, White Wolf stated that a new Vampire: The Masquerade project was coming and that more details would be revealed at Gen Con 2026.

A press release provided a bit of additional context. It confirms that the exact nature of the project will be unveiled at the event, an actual play will premiere at Gen Con showcasing the new project, there will be a "Future of White Wolf" panel at the convention, and that the project will be overseen by Creative Director Jess Lanzillo, a former employee of Wizards of the Coast.

Overall, the announcement does invite intrigue and speculation. Gen Con is one of the largest dedicated tabletop conventions in the United States, and Vampire: The Masquerade has been a persistent presence in the tabletop community. 

The announcement also coincides with White Wolf making several changes to the business side of the company. These changes have included the departure of brand manager Jason Carl, and the movement of all tabletop and IP-development projects in-house.

With this context, it can be speculated that the project may be a new edition of Vampire: The Masquerade. A clean break from the era of books developed under the tenure of Renegade Game Studios. The presser's slightly redesigned ankh logo, and the fact that the presser mentions playtesting, seem to confirm that the project is something for the TTRPG.

It may also be a further push into multimedia, given more recent World of Darkness projects. This includes the most recent season of Dimension 20, City Council of Darkness, using Vampire: The Masquerade V5, and the announcement of the Hunter: The Reckoning - Deathwish videogame by Teyon Interactive. 

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/news/white-wolf-announces-new-vampire-masquerade-project-coming-to-gen-con-2026
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Destruction AllStars Delisted and Servers Suddenly Taken Offline
item.content:encoded:
Update 5:20 PM Eastern: Sony has sent out an email about the Destruction AllStars delisting and server closure providing a bit more information. As stated in the original story, the multiplayer servers are offline as of today, and the game is delisted, but they provided a bit more information on singleplayer modes. Players will be able to play all Destruction AllStars singleplayer modes through November 28th, when the remaining servers will go offline. After that date, only arcade mode single player challenges will remain playable, although "functionality and player experience may be impacted" according to PlayStation.

Original story below

In the blink of an eye, it was over, and without warning PlayStation today brought an unceremonious end to the life of Destruction AllStars with a delisting and the announcement that it's servers were being taken offline.

Typically when games are taken offline there is a warning period, and a since of wind down as the developers tell the community the game is going away, but that was completely absent from Sony's shocking removal of Destruction AllStars. There is no announcement on the PlayStation Blog, nothing else, just an announcement to those who had a copy of it from purchasing it or from PS Plus and its complete lack of listing on the PlayStation Store.

First reported by Radec on X, we independently confirmed the game was no longer findable on the PlayStation store. There was a Game Service Shutdown message that reads as follows:

Multiplayer services for Destruction AllStars have been taken offline.

Destruction Points have been removed from sale on the PlayStation Store and can be redeemed until 25th November 2026. Arcade mode remains accessible for returning players.

The last bit does mean that the offline modes will at least remain available for people who own Destruction AllStars, and while the game may not have been beloved, there were surely a few fans of it out there. It's not been an easy life for the PlayStation 5 exclusive which was supposed to be a launch title but was delayed into 2021, with preorders being refunded as well.

Once it released, also on PlayStation Plus, it struggled to find an audience and within a month it had a major price cut as they attempted to interest players in the game, dropping the non-PS Plus price from $70 to $20. It had a little bit of ongoing content but didn't pick up any more as Sony moved on, leaving the game in a maintenance mode until today, with the last Twitter post on the game's official feed being in 2022. Now it joins Concord in the realm of dead Sony live service ambitions that litter the past 6 years.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/destruction-allstars-delisted-and-servers-suddenly-taken-offline
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
